title: oxalis corniculata var. javanica
intro: Oxalis corniculata var. javanica, also known as creeping woodsorrel, is a perennial plant in the wood sorrel family that is native to tropical Asia. It has been traditionally used in medicine for its anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and antifungal properties, which are attributed to its high concentration of oxalic acid and flavonoids. The health benefits of this plant include treating fever, digestive issues, and skin conditions, as well as exhibiting antimicrobial properties that can combat bacterial and fungal infections. In traditional herbal medicine, Oxalis corniculata var. javanica can be prepared into teas, infusions, or tinctures, which are often used to treat various health conditions and promote overall well-being.
